title: Fox Chess Academy: Strategic Pathfinding Lesson
description: An interactive educational scene where a fox teaches chess strategy by physically walking across a giant chessboard. The user selects chess pieces to learn about their movement patterns, then guides the fox along valid move paths by clicking highlighted squares, reinforcing spatial reasoning and chess fundamentals.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements an interactive chess education scene. Load both GLB assets using GLTFLoader from a CDN. Place the ABeautifulGame chess set scaled up 3x at the scene center so the board acts as a giant floor-level game board. Position the Fox model on the board scaled to fit roughly one square, playing its walk animation using AnimationMixer when moving. Setup: Use a PerspectiveCamera at position (0,12,14) looking at the board center. Add ambient light intensity 0.6, a directional light from (5,10,7) with intensity 1.0 casting shadows, and enable shadow maps on the renderer. Create an HTML overlay panel on the left showing the current lesson step and piece name. Interaction flow (7 steps with validation): Step 1: Display welcome message asking user to click any chess piece on the board. Step 2: On piece click via raycasting, highlight that piece with emissive glow (yellow emissive 0.3), display its name and movement rules in the overlay panel. Step 3: Generate and highlight valid destination squares for that piece type as green translucent plane meshes on the board grid. Validate that at least one valid square exists. Step 4: User clicks a highlighted valid square. Validate the click hits a valid square, reject clicks elsewhere with a red flash feedback. Step 5: Animate the fox walking from its current board position to the selected square using the Fox walk animation, interpolating position over 2 seconds along the path with TWEEN or manual lerp. Step 6: On arrival, stop walk animation, switch to fox survey/idle pose, increment a score counter shown in the overlay, and display a fun fact about that chess piece. Step 7: Prompt user to select another piece to continue learning. After completing 3 different piece lessons, show a congratulations screen with total score. Track which pieces have been studied to prevent repeats. Use OrbitControls with damping enabled, constrained to prevent going below the board. Render at 60fps with requestAnimationFrame. All UI text should use clean sans-serif font overlays positioned with CSS.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  currentStep: number,         // current lesson step (1-7)
  selectedPieceType: string,         // type of chess piece selected (pawn|rook|knight|bishop|queen|king)
  foxPositionX: number,         // fox X coordinate on board grid
  foxPositionZ: number,         // fox Z coordinate on board grid
  validSquaresCount: number,         // count of valid destination squares highlighted
  foxIsMoving: boolean,         // whether fox is currently animating movement
  destinationReached: boolean,         // whether fox reached clicked destination
  totalMovesCompleted: number,         // count of successful pathfinding moves
  pieceHighlighted: boolean,         // whether a chess piece is currently highlighted
  lessonComplete: boolean,         // whether all 7 steps completed successfully
}
